free_text: Activation cross-sections of proton induced reactions
on natHf in the 38-65 MeV energy range: Production of
172Lu and of 169Yb
main reference
Additional refs. for experimental details.
High purity natural hafnium foils 10 um from
Goodfellow, 99.5%.
Ti monitor foils 10.9 um and
Al 100 and 50 um energy degradation foils.
One stack consisted of a sequence of
Al, Dy, Al, Hf, Al, Ti, Be, Ti
targets repeated 25 times was bombarded for 3600 s
with a 65 MeV proton beam of 25 nA.
Chapter 4
The uncertainty of the beam energy in the
middle of each irradiated foil was estimated
combining the uncertainties of the energy degradation
depending on the thickness of the target foils in the
stack and the energy straggling.
The uncertainty on the cross-section was
estimated by taking the square root of the sum in
quadrature of all individual contributions, supposing
equal sensitivities for the different parameters.
The main error components:
surface density of target nuclei (5%)
incident charged particle flux (8%)
detector efficiency  (5%)
nuclear data (5%)
statistical uncertainty (1.-15.%)
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TALYS, EMPIRE
and ALICE.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
